name: Maxine's Cranberry Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop cranberries in a food processor or blender.
2. Place them in a large lidded jar.
3. Add sugar and gin to the jar.
4. Store for 14 days, turning or shaking the container daily.
5. Strain off the cranberries; reserve for dessert topping or use in cranberry nut bread (the baking removes most of the alcohol).
6. Decant the liqueur into an appropriate bottle.
7. Keeps indefinitely. This liqueur is perfect for holiday gatherings and will keep you warm regardless of the weather.
